sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(111) — Pole Creek and tributaries (Placer Co.)
sources.quotedText: (111) Pole Creek and tributaries (Placer Co.). Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: Pole Creek Protected! — Truckee Donner Land Trust
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 234499, "Pole Creek", Stream, Placer Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":234499,"gaz_name":"Pole Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Placer"}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13381306.327, 4755656.179) converts to lon -120.206320, lat 39.236856, near the creek's mouth at the Truckee River just north of Olympic Valley (matches Truckee Donner Land Trust's description of the Pole Creek watershed as "located just north of Olympic Valley" and linking "the Sierra Crest above to the Truckee River below"); corrects the prior file coordinates (-120.2210, 39.2280), which were ~1.6 km from this GNIS-recorded mouth point. Second point (Web Mercator -13387676.452, 4755217.011) converts to lon -120.263544, lat 39.233800, corresponding to the creek's headwater/source end.

parameters.note: Pole Creek and all of its tributaries in Placer County are closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. This water is closed to all fishing all year, so the license requirement is moot here, but is included for statewide-standard completeness.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
